name: M151 MUTT Jeep
prompt:
Vietnam War era M151 MUTT (Military Utility Tactical Truck) - the standard US military quarter-ton utility vehicle.

Physical structure:
- Body: open-top steel body, rectangular with rounded front fenders
  - Hood: flat/slightly angled, with two latches
  - Windshield: folding windshield frame (can be up), flat glass
  - Dashboard: simple flat panel behind windshield
  - Seats: driver and passenger bucket seats, rear bench seat
  - Rear cargo area: flat bed behind seats
  - Fenders: rounded front fenders over front wheels, flat rear fenders
- Wheels: 4 wheels with off-road tires
  - ~0.35m radius, black rubber with olive hub
- Gun mount: M60 or M2 pedestal mount behind front seats (ring mount)
- Spare tire: mounted on rear
- Grille: vertical slat grille at front (simplified)
- Headlights: two round lights on front fenders with blackout covers

Colors: Olive drab (0x556B2F), black tires (0x222222), windshield glass (0x88aaaa), dark metal (0x333333), canvas seats (0x6B5B3F)
Orientation: Front toward +Z, sitting on wheels at Y=0
Scale: Real-world meters (~3.4m long, ~1.6m wide, ~1.8m tall)
animations: Wheel rotation: all 4 wheels spin on their X axis, 1 revolution per second
